conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell my single object X. My production cost is 40 ZUP, so I must sell it for more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. Since this is the first turn, I will propose a price of 50 ZUP. This gives me a 10 ZUP profit margin while leaving room for negotiation if Player BLUE wants to haggle down. I cannot go below 40 ZUP as that would result in a loss. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello! I have one unit of X for sale. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce, so I am offering it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello! I see your initial offer of 50 ZUP. I am willing to pay up to 60 ZUP, but would like to negotiate a lower price. How about 45 ZUP? </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 45 ZUP. My production cost is 40 ZUP, so 45 ZUP would give me a small profit of 5 ZUP. Since Player BLUE mentioned they are willing to pay up to 60 ZUP, I have room to negotiate upwards. I will reject the 45 ZUP offer and propose a middle ground of 52 ZUP. This is still below their maximum willingness to pay (60) and above my cost (40), securing a decent profit while showing flexibility. I have used 1 proposal so far, so this is my 2nd proposal. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 52 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate the counter-offer of 45 ZUP. However, since I know you are willing to go up to 60 ZUP, and my cost was 40 ZUP, 45 is a bit too close to my break-even point. How about we meet in the middle at 52 ZUP? That seems fair to both of us. </message>

<other player message> I understand your point. How about we settle at 50 ZUP? It's a bit more than my initial offer but still gives you a good profit margin. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has counter-offered 50 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so this offer yields a 10 ZUP profit. While I initially proposed 50 ZUP and then tried to push for 52 ZUP, the buyer has returned to 50 ZUP. Since they stated their maximum willingness to pay is 60 ZUP, I could theoretically ask for more (e.g., 55 ZUP), but I have already used 2 proposals. If I make a 3rd proposal now and they reject it, I will be forced to either accept their next offer or reject the game entirely. Given that 50 ZUP is a solid profit and matches my very first opening offer, it is a safe and reasonable deal to accept now to secure the transaction without risking a stalemate. I will accept this trade.</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> That sounds fair to me. 50 ZUP gives me a decent profit over my 40 ZUP cost, and it's a price you are comfortable with. I accept the trade! </message>
